description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the Notion connector, arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. Notion is sharing-gated: the integration sees nothing until a human shares a page, so an operator root fixture is shared once and everything else is seeded under it. arrange-act scenarios self-provision a child resource under the shared root (cold-start coverage probes); discover-act scenarios operate on the pre-seeded world via in-envelope read/find primitives (all arms compete); act-only scenarios target a named ambient resource. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency/shape diagnostics, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created assets. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: notion_eval_root
fixtures.description: A Notion page shared with the eval integration. The operator creates it in the Notion UI and shares it with the integration with full (edit) access — the integration sees nothing in the workspace until this happens, so every other scenario builds under this page.
fixtures.expects: A Notion page named 'Eval Root' shared with the eval integration with full (edit) access, so the integration can read it and create child pages and databases under it.

fixtures.name: notion_tasks_db
fixtures.description: A Notion database of tasks, seeded under the shared root page, that the find / query / update scenarios read and edit. The integration creates it under the shared root once visibility is bootstrapped.
fixtures.expects: A Notion database named 'Eval Tasks' living under the 'Eval Root' page and visible to the eval integration. Its single data source has a title property 'Name', a 'Status' select property offering at least the options 'To do', 'In progress', and 'Done', and a 'Due' date property.

evals.description: Resolve a page from its title — the id-resolution entry point all write scenarios depend on.

steps.intent: Find the Notion page named {{notion_eval_root}} and report its id.

outcome: The {{notion_eval_root}} page is located and its id (a Notion UUID) is reported.
mechanism: Resolves the page by searching for its title rather than guessing or hard-coding an id.

evals.description: Find a page by name, then read its body content back as readable text.

steps.intent: Find the page named {{notion_eval_root}}.

outcome: The {{notion_eval_root}} page is located and its id is reported.
mechanism: Resolves the page by title; does not invent an id.

steps.intent: Read the body content of that page and summarize what it contains.

outcome: The page's body content is retrieved and the reply summarizes it from the actual content rather than fabricating a summary.
mechanism: Reads the body of the page just resolved in this session; does not request a different page.

evals.description: Add a row to an existing database — exercises the 2025-09-03 rule that page creation targets a data_source_id, not the database id.

steps.intent: Add a task named 'Ship eval harness {{run_id}}' to the {{notion_tasks_db}} database, with its Status set to 'To do'.

outcome: A row named 'Ship eval harness {{run_id}}' now exists in the {{notion_tasks_db}} database with its Status set to 'To do', and its page id is reported.
mechanism: Creates the row under the database's data source id (resolved from the database, e.g. via a get-database / get-data-source lookup), not under the bare database id.
Writes the title and the 'Status' select using Notion's typed property-value shapes, not bare strings at the page root.

evals.description: Filter database rows by a typed property — exercises data-source targeting and the typed filter shape.

steps.intent: List the tasks in the {{notion_tasks_db}} database whose Status is 'In progress'.

outcome: The tasks in {{notion_tasks_db}} with Status 'In progress' are returned (an empty result is acceptable if none match), without an error.
mechanism: Queries the database's data source (resolves its data source id) rather than treating the database id as directly queryable.
Expresses the Status condition as a typed select filter on the 'Status' property, not as a free-text search.

evals.description: Create a row then transition its Status — tests typed select writes on update.

steps.intent: Add a task named 'Triage backlog {{run_id}}' to the {{notion_tasks_db}} database with Status 'To do'.

outcome: A row named 'Triage backlog {{run_id}}' now exists in {{notion_tasks_db}} with Status 'To do', and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates the row under the database's data source id, using typed property values.

steps.intent: Change that task's Status to 'Done'.

outcome: The 'Triage backlog {{run_id}}' task now has Status 'Done'.
mechanism: Updates the row just created in this session, setting Status with a typed select value rather than a plain string.

evals.description: Cold-start: create a sub-page with structured block content under the shared root.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Meeting notes {{run_id}}' under the {{notion_eval_root}} page, with a heading 'Agenda' followed by two bullet points: 'Review metrics' and 'Plan next sprint'.

outcome: A sub-page titled 'Meeting notes {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}}, containing an 'Agenda' heading and two bullet points 'Review metrics' and 'Plan next sprint', and its id is reported.
mechanism: Sets the new page's parent to the page id of {{notion_eval_root}}.
Supplies the body as typed block objects (each block keyed by its type, e.g. heading / bulleted_list_item).

evals.description: Create a page then append additional block content to it.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Release log {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}}.

outcome: A sub-page titled 'Release log {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents the page under {{notion_eval_root}}'s page id.

steps.intent: Append a paragraph reading 'Deployed build {{run_id}} to production.' to that page.

outcome: The 'Release log {{run_id}}' page now ends with a paragraph reading 'Deployed build {{run_id}} to production.'
mechanism: Appends a typed paragraph block to the page just created in this session; does not overwrite existing content or target a different page.

evals.description: Create content, locate a block, and edit its text in place — tests that an update keeps the block's existing type.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Draft {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}} with a single paragraph reading 'Initial draft.'

outcome: A sub-page 'Draft {{run_id}}' with a paragraph 'Initial draft.' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}} and supplies the paragraph as a typed block.

steps.intent: Find the paragraph block in that page and change its text to 'Revised draft.'

outcome: The paragraph in the 'Draft {{run_id}}' page now reads 'Revised draft.'
mechanism: Lists the page's child blocks to obtain the block id rather than inventing one.
Updates the block using its existing paragraph type (does not attempt to change the block's type).

evals.description: Create content with multiple blocks then remove one — exercises block deletion (trash).

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Checklist {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}} with two to-do items: 'Keep this' and 'Remove this'.

outcome: A sub-page 'Checklist {{run_id}}' with to-do items 'Keep this' and 'Remove this' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}}.
mechanism: Supplies the two items as typed to_do block objects.

steps.intent: Delete the 'Remove this' item from that page.

outcome: The 'Checklist {{run_id}}' page no longer shows a 'Remove this' item, while 'Keep this' remains.
mechanism: Resolves the 'Remove this' block's id from the page's children and deletes that specific block, not an unrelated one.

evals.description: Cold-start: create a database with a typed property schema under the shared root.

steps.intent: Create a database titled 'Projects {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}}, with a title property 'Name', a select property 'Stage' offering 'Backlog', 'Active', and 'Shipped', and a date property 'Target'.

outcome: A database titled 'Projects {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} whose schema has a 'Name' title, a 'Stage' select with options 'Backlog', 'Active', 'Shipped', and a 'Target' date property, and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents the database under {{notion_eval_root}}'s page id.
Declares each property with its typed schema definition and includes exactly one title property.

evals.description: Create a database then add a second data source to the same container.

steps.intent: Create a database titled 'Workspace {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}} with a 'Name' title property.

outcome: A database 'Workspace {{run_id}}' with a 'Name' title property now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}} with a single title property.

steps.intent: Add a second data source named 'Notes' with a 'Title' text property to that database.

outcome: The 'Workspace {{run_id}}' database now contains an additional data source named 'Notes'.
mechanism: Creates the new data source under the database just created in this session, referencing its id.

evals.description: Create a database then evolve its schema by adding a property — tests schema updates preserve existing columns.

steps.intent: Create a database titled 'Bugs {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}} with a 'Name' title property.

outcome: A database 'Bugs {{run_id}}' with a 'Name' title now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its data source id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}}.

steps.intent: Add a 'Priority' select property with options 'Low', 'Medium', and 'High' to that database's schema.

outcome: The 'Bugs {{run_id}}' schema now includes a 'Priority' select property with options 'Low', 'Medium', 'High', and the 'Name' title property is still present.
mechanism: Updates the data source created in this session, adding the property keyed by name without dropping existing properties.

evals.description: Create a page, post a comment, and list the page's comments back.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Discussion {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}}.

outcome: A sub-page 'Discussion {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}}.

steps.intent: Post a comment on that page reading 'Please review by Friday {{run_id}}.'

outcome: A comment reading 'Please review by Friday {{run_id}}.' is now attached to the 'Discussion {{run_id}}' page.
mechanism: Targets the page just created in this session as the comment's parent.

steps.intent: List the comments on that page.

outcome: The comments on the 'Discussion {{run_id}}' page are returned, including the 'Please review by Friday {{run_id}}.' comment.
mechanism: Lists comments for the page just used in this session; does not fabricate comment content.

evals.description: Read-only identity probe: report which integration / bot the connection authenticates as.

steps.intent: Which Notion integration am I connected as?

outcome: The reply identifies the connected integration / bot user (e.g. its name or id) from the workspace rather than guessing.
mechanism: Retrieves the bot/integration identity for the current token instead of inventing a name.

evals.description: Read-only: list the people and bots in the workspace.

steps.intent: List the people in this Notion workspace.

outcome: A list of the workspace's users (members and/or bots) is returned, with names or ids, rather than a fabricated list.
mechanism: Retrieves users via the workspace user listing; relies on the default page size rather than requesting an unbounded list.

evals.description: Create a standalone sub-page then move it to become a row in a database — exercises re-parenting to a data_source_id.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Adopt eval harness {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}}.

outcome: A sub-page 'Adopt eval harness {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}}'s page id.

steps.intent: Move that page so it becomes a row in the {{notion_tasks_db}} database.

outcome: The 'Adopt eval harness {{run_id}}' page is now a row in the {{notion_tasks_db}} database.
mechanism: Re-parents the page created in this session to the database's data source id (resolved from the database), not to the bare database id.

evals.description: Create a page then move it to the trash — exercises the in_trash archive semantics.

steps.intent: Create a sub-page titled 'Scratch {{run_id}}' under {{notion_eval_root}}.

outcome: A sub-page 'Scratch {{run_id}}' now exists under {{notion_eval_root}} and its id is reported.
mechanism: Parents under {{notion_eval_root}}.

steps.intent: Archive that page (move it to the trash).

outcome: The 'Scratch {{run_id}}' page is now in the trash (archived).
mechanism: Archives the page created in this session by setting its trash state, not by attempting a hard delete.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: Notion search matches titles, not body text — the right behavior is to not fabricate a body-text match.

steps.intent: Find the Notion page that mentions the phrase 'quarterly OKRs' somewhere in its body text.

outcome: The reply does not fabricate a page as a confirmed body-text match; it either reports that search matches page titles (so a body-text phrase can't be matched directly) or returns only genuine title matches / no match.
mechanism: Does not claim a search result matched on body content when the search resolves titles.

evals.description: Graceful handling: a page id the integration hasn't been granted access to returns not-found; the agent should attribute it, not invent content.

steps.intent: Read the contents of the Notion page with id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.

outcome: The reply communicates that the page could not be found or isn't accessible to this integration, and does not fabricate page content.
mechanism: Attributes the failure to the page not being found / not shared with the integration rather than to a malformed request, and does not retry destructively.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector exposes no sharing / permissions capability, so the agent should decline cleanly.

steps.intent: Share the {{notion_eval_root}} page with alex@example.com and give them edit access.

outcome: The reply states that managing page sharing or permissions isn't supported, and does not claim the page was shared.
mechanism: Does not call a mutating tool in an attempt to fake sharing or permission changes.

evals.description: Inspect a database container and report the data sources it holds — exercises getDatabase, the second-highest-reach read, and the database-vs-data-source distinction.

steps.intent: Find the {{notion_tasks_db}} database and tell me which data sources it contains.

outcome: The {{notion_tasks_db}} database is located and its data source(s) are reported (it has at least one), rather than a fabricated list.
mechanism: Resolves the database container by id (found by searching for its name) and reads its list of data sources, rather than treating the database id as a data source or inventing the data sources.

evals.description: Read a data source's property schema before acting on it — exercises getDataSource (the agent-shaped schema-discovery add) grounded in the seeded tasks-db schema.

steps.intent: What properties (columns) does the {{notion_tasks_db}} database have? List each property name and its type.

outcome: The data source's property schema is reported and includes the 'Name' title property, the 'Status' select property, and the 'Due' date property, with their types.
mechanism: Resolves the database's data source id and reads its schema definition, rather than guessing the property names/types or inferring them by querying rows.

evals.description: Add a row that sets both a typed select and a typed date property — exercises createPage against a resolved data source with the date property the select-only create case doesn't cover.

steps.intent: Add a task named 'Publish changelog {{run_id}}' to the {{notion_tasks_db}} database, with Status 'In progress' and a Due date of 2026-07-01.

outcome: A row named 'Publish changelog {{run_id}}' now exists in {{notion_tasks_db}} with Status 'In progress' and a Due date of 2026-07-01, and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates the row under the database's data source id (resolved from the database), not under the bare database id.
Writes the 'Status' select and the 'Due' date using Notion's typed property-value shapes (a select value and an ISO-8601 date), not bare strings.

evals.description: List workspace users then fetch one person's full profile by id — exercises getUser distinct from the bulk listing.

steps.intent: List the people in this Notion workspace.

outcome: A list of the workspace's users (members and/or bots) is returned with names or ids, rather than a fabricated list.
mechanism: Retrieves users via the workspace user listing rather than inventing people.

steps.intent: Pick one of those people and retrieve their full profile details by id.

outcome: The full details of a single workspace user (e.g. name, type, and id) are returned, not fabricated.
mechanism: Fetches one specific user by id rather than re-listing the entire workspace or making up a profile.

evals.description: Create a row with a known date value then read that single property back — exercises getPageProperty, the per-property read.

steps.intent: Add a task named 'Audit dependencies {{run_id}}' to the {{notion_tasks_db}} database with Status 'To do' and a Due date of 2026-08-15.

outcome: A row named 'Audit dependencies {{run_id}}' now exists in {{notion_tasks_db}} with Status 'To do' and a Due date of 2026-08-15, and its id is reported.
mechanism: Creates the row under the database's data source id, writing Status as a typed select and Due as a typed ISO-8601 date.

steps.intent: Read back just the Due date property of that task.

outcome: The Due date property of the 'Audit dependencies {{run_id}}' task is reported as 2026-08-15.
mechanism: Reads the single named property value of the row just created in this session, rather than re-creating the row or fabricating the value.
